'''Paul Aiton''' (born 1985) is a Papua New Guinean former professional [[rugby league]] footballer  who is the current head-coach of the [[PNG Hunters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] and assistant-coach of the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|PNG Kumuls]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://postcourier.com.pg/kumuls-name-squad-for-pacific-test/ | title=Kumuls name squad for Pacific Test | date=15 June 2022 }}</ref>

A [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] international representative {{rlp|hk}}, Aiton played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

== Early life ==
Born in [[Mount Hagen]], Papua New Guinea to a Papua New Guinean mother, Anne, and an Australian father, John, Aiton moved to [[Caboolture, Queensland]], Australia with his family at a young age. He played his junior rugby league for the Caboolture Snakes and attended Morayfield State Primary School before joining the [[Wests Panthers]] in [[Brisbane]], where he was signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]] in 2003.<ref name=wantok>{{cite web|date=25 September 2007|title=Paul Aiton, our wantok|url=http://postcourier.com.pg/20070925/sport02.htm|publisher=Post Courier|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518023220/http://postcourier.com.pg/20070925/sport02.htm|archive-date=18 May 2011}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Penrith Panthers===
After playing for Penrith's second-tier team, the [[St Mary's Cougars]], in the [[NSWRL Premier League]], Aiton was called upon to make his NRL début on 23 April 2006, starting at [[hooker (rugby league)|hooker]] for Penrith in their round 8 match against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], as a result of an injury sustained by [[Craig Gower]].<ref name=wantok /> Aiton made 13 further appearances from the bench in 2006, and was named Penrith's [[Penrith Panthers Honours#Club awards|Ben Alexander Rookie of the Year]].<ref>{{cite web|date=5 October 2006|title=Wesser named Penrith's best|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2006-10-05/wesser-named-penriths-best/1278720|publisher=ABC|access-date=25 October 2006}}</ref> He played a total of 72 games, scoring 8 tries, for the Panthers between 2006 and 2009.

===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
Aiton signed a 2-year deal with Cronulla in 2010 and went on to make 36 appearances, scoring 5 tries. At the end of the 2011 season it was announced he was to leave Cronulla to go play in the [[Super League]].

Following his departure, he was one of seventeen Sharks players found guilty of using illegal substances under the club's [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga|2011 supplements program]]. When the case was eventually concluded in 2016, Aiton had a twelve-month suspension recorded against his name, although he served none of the time due to backdating of the sanction.<ref name="forgottenfivepenalties">{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/32885375/sharks-finalise-asada-deal/#page1|title=Sharks finalise ASADA deal|publisher=Yahoo!7|first=Steve|last=Zemek|date=13 October 2016|access-date=13 October 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161013150138/https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/32885375/sharks-finalise-asada-deal/#page1|archive-date=13 October 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
Aiton transferred to [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in England on a two-year contract from 2012.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41923|title=Aiton joins Wakefield|date=17 December 2011|work=rleague.com|access-date=17 December 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120103113814/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41923|archive-date=3 January 2012|df=dmy-all}}</ref> He made 47 appearances for them in 2 years scoring 7 tries.

===Leeds Rhinos===
In November 2013, Aiton signed a three-year contract with the [[Leeds Rhinos]] starting in 2014.<ref>{{cite news|date=5 November 2013|title=Leeds Rhinos sign Paul Aiton from Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24804735|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=8 November 2013}}</ref>

He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=12 November 2019}}</ref> Leeds' first in over a decade.

He missed part of the 2014 season due to medication he was taking that prevented him from playing. Aiton was injured towards the end of the 2015 season and missed out on winning the Treble with Leeds. He left making 47 appearances scoring 3 times.

===Catalans Dragons===
On 5 August 2015, Aiton decided not to sign a new deal with the Leeds Rhinos, instead moving on to play for France's biggest rugby league franchise the [[Catalans Dragons]]. Aiton said “I will always be honoured to have played for Leeds Rhinos but the opportunity to move to France from next season and be part of the Dragons was something that excited me at this stage in my career." He signed a three-year deal with the Dragons beginning in the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Catalans Dragons sign Paul Aiton from Leeds Rhinos
| publisher = SkySports
| date = 2015-08-05
| url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/9935615/catalans-dragons-sign-paul-aiton-from-leeds-rhinos
| access-date = 2015-08-09}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2004, Aiton was selected to play for [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in a non-Test match.<ref name=wantok />

In 2007, Aiton played 3 matches for PNG during their end of season tour of Europe.

Aiton was named in the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team#Current squad|Papua New Guinea squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx|title=News : PNG name preliminary squad|date=2010-04-10|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100410150913/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx|archive-date=2010-04-10|access-date=2018-03-23}}</ref>

He captained [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[2010 Four Nations]] tournament.

He again played for Papua New Guinea in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] and [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Coaching career==
In 2021, Aiton was appointed assistant coach of the [[Papua New Guinea Hunters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Edward|first=Colleen|date=12 January 2021|title=PNG Hunters name assistant coach and final 2021 squad|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2021/01/12/png-hunters-name-assistant-coach-and-final-2021-squad/|publisher=Queensland Rugby League|access-date=12 April 2021}}</ref>

On 17 Nov 2023, Aiton was appointed head-coach of the [[Papua New Guinea Hunters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Darbyshire |first=Drew|title=PNG Hunters appoint head coach for 2024 in shape of former NRL and Super League star|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/png-hunters-appoint-head-coach-for-2024-in-shape-of-former-nrl-and-super-league-star-paul-aiton|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2023}}</ref>
